You know your workflows best. Show our team how you use Cowork so we can make it work better for you!

If you're in marketing, sales, finance, legal, or any non-engineering role, sign up here for a 30 min screen share with our team: https://t.co/kvc8uYzcNL
remarks.translation:
中文释义：你最了解自己的工作流程。向我们的团队展示你如何使用 Cowork，以便我们让它更好地为你服务！如果你从事市场营销、销售、财务、法务或任何非工程类岗位，请在此处报名，与我们的团队进行 30 分钟的屏幕共享：https://t.co/kvc8uYzcNL

保留信息：30m
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
The bigger payoff comes when fixing and maintaining happens in the background and your teams can focus on building. That's when you start doing things that weren't even in range before.

Anthropic is on step 3 and pushing toward 4. Personally, I just hit level 4.

Curious where you are -- what step is your team on?
remarks.translation: 当修复和维护在后台悄然进行，团队得以专注于构建时，更大的回报便随之而来。那时，你就能着手处理以往遥不可及的事务。Anthropic正处于第3步，并正向第4步迈进。就我个人而言，我刚达到第4级。好奇你处于哪个阶段——你的团队正处在哪一步？
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text: Once your teams are bought in, how do you track it? Usage is worth watching (e.g. a dashboard), but it measures activity, not return. A better question: would you have spent engineering effort on this anyway? If yes, how much and what would it have cost in manual eng-hours? That's your return.
remarks.translation: 一旦你的团队接受了这个方案，如何追踪效果？使用情况值得关注（例如通过仪表盘），但它衡量的是活跃度，而非回报。更好的问题是：你们原本是否会为此投入工程资源？如果是，投入多少，折算成人工工时成本是多少？那才是你的回报。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
In practice that means giving Claude ways to verify its own work end to end. It means enabling auto mode for permissions, defaulting on automated code review and security review, and using interfaces that let you manage multiple agents at once (Agent view in CLI, Desktop app, iOS and Android apps, Tag).

To get to higher levels it means /loop, /batch, dynamic workflows, and worktree isolation for subagents. It's not about a single feature, but rather using the right features with the right guardrails that enable Claude to automate entire classes of work in a way that your team can trust the output.
remarks.translation: 实践中，这意味着让 Claude 具备端到端验证自身工作的能力。具体包括启用权限的自动模式，默认开启自动化代码审查和安全审查，并使用能同时管理多个智能体的界面（如命令行工具中的智能体视图、桌面应用、iOS 和安卓应用、标签功能）。要达到更高层级，则需要借助 /loop、/batch、动态工作流以及子智能体的工作树隔离。这并非依赖单一功能，而是通过搭配恰当的功能与防护机制，让 Claude 能够自动化处理整类工作，使团队可以信赖其产出。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
Evening! We’ve gotten lots of great feedback on the new ChatGPT desktop app (which we didn't get totally quite right on the first try), and as a result, we've made some changes.

1/ ChatGPT conversation history and projects are now visible in the sidebar. Also, your Chat and Work history now sync across web, mobile, and desktop. Local tasks still stay on your computer.
2/ You can now easily switch between Chat and Work modes inside ChatGPT on desktop, which is now also consistent with how it shows on web and mobile.
3/ Nothing is changing for users on Codex mode. It's still the OG and best at what it does.

And overall we're continuing to fix paper cuts and improve performance, reliability, and efficiency.

Keep up the feedback, hope you like the updates!
remarks.translation: 晚上好！我们收到了大量关于新版ChatGPT桌面应用（首次尝试确实不够完美）的反馈，基于这些建议我们做了一些调整。1/ 现在侧边栏可以显示ChatGPT对话记录和项目了。同时，聊天和工作记录会在网页端、移动端和桌面端同步。本地任务仍保留在您的电脑上。2/ 桌面端ChatGPT现在可以轻松切换聊天和工作模式，这与网页端和移动端的显示方式保持一致。3/ Codex模式的用户不受任何影响。它依然是原汁原味、表现最佳的版本。整体上我们持续修复细节问题，提升性能、可靠性和运行效率。请继续反馈，希望你们喜欢这些更新！
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Amjad Masad
remarks.role: ceo replit. civilizationist
remarks.text:
If you want to play my chess engine (WIP): https://t.co/4WJ9SYmr1o

It already seems to perform better than frontier models on chess.

It’s fine-tuned on 2M stockfish-labeled positions, then short GRPO RL pass.

Documentation/tutorial with all the experiments and annotated code. https://t.co/INdb7rojHU
remarks.translation:
中文释义：想和我的国际象棋引擎（开发中）对弈：https://t.co/4WJ9SYmr1o 它目前的表现似乎已优于前沿模型。该引擎基于200万Stockfish标注局面进行微调，随后经过简短GRPO强化学习训练。附有完整实验记录与代码注释的文档/教程。https://t.co/INdb7rojHU

保留信息：2m
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
Kimi K3 is the best performing model on https://t.co/aporqgIfIh, ahead of Fable, reaching a comparable success rate in less time.

This is the first time that an open model is ahead of all proprietary ones for this comprehensive web engineering benchmark.

Notes:

▪️ Benchmarks don’t always tell the full story, although this is important signal, adding to mounting evidence that this could be a breakthrough moment for open models 

▪️ No model as of yet has reached 100% completion on this set of evals. The top performer peaks at 92% and 96% “with help”
remarks.translation:
中文释义：Kimi K3 是 https://t.co/aporqgIfIh 上表现最佳的模型，领先于 Fable，以更短时间达到了可比的完成率。这是开放模型首次在这一综合性网页工程基准测试中超越所有闭源模型。备注：▪️ 基准测试并不总能反映全貌，但这仍是一个重要信号，进一步佐证了这可能是开放模型迎来突破的时刻 ▪️ 目前尚无模型在这套评估中达到 100% 完成率。表现最佳的模型峰值完成率为 92%，在“辅助下”可达 96%

保留信息：https://t.co/aporqgIfIh,

remarks.text:
A lot of people assume that as you get more successful, the LESS you get said No to

This is actually false if you are operating at the edge of your ambition

At higher stakes, you get told No a lot. I see this with all founders who are punching up

I got told No 5 times today. https://t.co/d2Ws69vFQM

remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
Open-weight models like Kimi and GLM will cause a complete rethink of the enterprise AI stack. 
If you are running an enterprise, you need to maximimize model optionality.
Here are 3 things you should be doing: 

1. Evals - build rigorous evals representative of your use case 
(a) Regression evals - tablestakes features that should always work with high reliability
(b) Aspirational / hill-climbing evals - harder use cases that the best model for your price point struggles to solve. So you scaffold it with prompting, context management and other techniques to overcome weaknesses or wait for a better model
These evals should be easy and quick to run. Eval velocity is a competitive advantage.

2. Model routing - Model selection is about tradiing off quality/cost/latency for your use case. If you have good evals, you know which models to route traffic to for different use cases.
This is something you ideally build yourself cos nobody understands your business and users like you do. 
There are off the shelf routers, but I haven't seen one I would recommend yet.
 
3. Model-agnostic harness - your system should never know which model is behind the API call.
This means your harness normalizes prompt structure, context management, tool definitions, and output parsing across models, making it easy to switch... once your evals pass.
remarks.translation:
Kimi、GLM 这类开放权重模型，将促使企业 AI 技术栈全面重塑。若你经营一家企业，必须最大化模型选择的灵活性。以下是三项应立即采取的行动：

1. **评估体系** - 构建能精准反映业务场景的严格评估机制  
   （a）**回归评估** - 确保基础功能始终以高可靠性运行  
   （b）**进阶/攀登式评估** - 针对当前价位下最优模型仍难解决的复杂用例，通过提示工程、上下文管理及其他技术手段弥补短板，或等待更优模型出现  
   这些评估必须能快速便捷地执行。评估迭代速度本身就是竞争优势。

2. **模型路由** - 模型选择本质是在质量、成本、延迟之间为业务场景寻找平衡点。完善的评估体系能让你明确不同场景该调用哪个模型。这最好由企业自主构建，因为没人比你更懂自己的业务和用户。市面上虽有现成路由方案，但我尚未见到值得推荐的。

3. **模型无关型架构** - 系统绝不应感知 API 调用背后的具体模型。这意味着你的架构需统一不同模型的提示结构、上下文管理、工具定义和输出解析，确保评估通过后即可无缝切换。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
I’m excited to welcome two legends of developer tools, Pete Hunt (@floydophone) and Nick Schrock (@schrockn), to Vercel.

Pete was one of the pioneers of @reactjs at Meta. He made an early bet to power Instagram Web with ⚛️ React, evangelizing it internally and externally. He will be running Frameworks and leading @nextjs. I couldn’t imagine a better person to lead React’s most popular framework to even greater heights.

Nick co-invented @graphql, solving some of the gnarliest data infrastructure and access issues at Facebook scale, with a delightful developer experience. He will be working on Agentic Developer Experience, solving the problem of enabling the next billion agents and leading the way to a future of self-improving software.

It’s a dream-come-true for a founder of a startup to welcome engineering minds of this caliber who are also wonderful humans. You probably want to work with them, and they’re hiring 😁. Their DMs are open, from job applications to bug reports!
remarks.translation: 很高兴欢迎两位开发者工具领域的传奇人物——Pete Hunt（@floydophone）和Nick Schrock（@schrockn）加入Vercel。Pete是Meta旗下@reactjs的早期开拓者之一。他曾大胆押注用⚛️ React驱动Instagram网页版，并在公司内外大力推广这项技术。他将负责Frameworks部门并领导@nextjs团队。我想不出还有谁能比他更适合带领React最受欢迎的框架迈向更高峰。Nick是@graphql的联合发明人，在Facebook规模下解决了最棘手的数据基础设施与访问难题，同时打造了令人愉悦的开发者体验。他将投身Agentic Developer Experience领域，致力于解决赋能下一代十亿级智能体的难题，引领通往自我进化软件的未来。作为初创公司创始人，能迎来如此卓越的工程头脑与美好人格的伙伴，堪称梦想成真。你大概会想和他们共事——他们正在招人😁。无论是求职申请还是提交bug，欢迎随时私信联系！
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
It’s truly wild that we’re getting this level of performance from open models. Congrats to Kimi team on this.

Every time we lower the cost of frontier intelligence, the use-cases that enterprises can take on just go up. There’s a tremendous amount of workflows that enterprises would love to deploy that are only gated by the cost of tokens.

Importantly for the startup ecosystem, the combined breakthroughs from open and closed labs enable a ton of value to accrue to the layer, which can leverage a variety of models to complete full tasks for customers.

This diversity of models and approaches means that the applied AI layer can tune models to their workflows and route intelligence appropriately. Huge win for all.
remarks.translation: 开源模型能达到这种性能水平，实在令人惊叹。祝贺Kimi团队取得这一成就。每当我们将前沿智能的成本降低一分，企业能落地的应用场景就会随之扩展。目前有大量企业渴望部署的工作流程，其唯一门槛就是代币成本。对初创生态尤为关键的是，开放与封闭实验室的共同突破，使得大量价值得以沉淀到应用层——该层能灵活调用多种模型，为客户完成完整任务。这种模型与方法的多样性，意味着应用AI层可以针对工作流调校模型，并合理分配智能资源。这对所有人都是重大利好。

remarks.name: Dan Shipper
remarks.role: ceo every
remarks.text:
not surprising that @OpenAI is firing on all cylinders right now, and it’s an unbelievably interesting story

- they launched GPT-5 summer of 2025 and positioned it as a pair programmer. we wrote at the time @every that they completely missed the new agentic coding that was starting to happen inside of Claude Code. they bet on agentic coding in the browser / vms and vibe coding in ChatGPT but it was too early

- a small team broke off and began working on a separate Codex model line and product. didn’t have to serve the gigantic customer base of ChatGPT, and by November / December of 2025 with 5.3 it was clear they were starting to make something good and the progress was fast 

- Codex Desktop app launched in Feb and was just clearly superior. there’s a weird late comer advantage sometimes in AI because you get to skip to what works instead of your product having the scars of new capability improvements being bolted on every 3 months 

- Codex started getting popular it was clear it was a thing, and needed to get merged back in. Which they did, imo quite well—a super complicated thing that would’ve been very easy to screw up. 

most companies try to disrupt themselves and fail. OpenAI somehow figured out how to disrupt their main product, and then merge it back in seamlessly. incredible aura
remarks.translation:
中文释义：毫不意外，@OpenAI 现在正全面发力，而且这故事简直精彩得难以置信——他们在2025年夏天推出了GPT-5，并将其定位为结对编程工具。我们当时在@every 写道，他们完全错过了Claude Code内部正在兴起的新型智能体编程。他们押注了浏览器/虚拟机中的智能体编程以及ChatGPT中的氛围编程，但为时过早——一个小团队脱离出来，开始独立开发Codex模型系列和产品。他们无需服务ChatGPT庞大的客户群，到2025年11月/12月，随着5.3版本的推出，很明显他们开始做出了一些好东西，而且进展神速——Codex桌面应用于2月发布，明显更胜一筹。在AI领域，有时会有一种奇怪的后发优势，因为你可以直接跳到有效的方案，而不必让产品背负每三个月就硬加上新功能改进的伤痕——Codex开始流行起来，显然它已经成了气候，需要重新整合回去。他们确实这么做了，在我看来做得相当出色——这是一件极其复杂、很容易搞砸的事情。大多数公司试图自我颠覆却以失败告终。OpenAI不知怎么找到了颠覆自己主打产品的方法，然后又无缝地将其整合回去。不可思议的气场。

保留信息：3m
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Sam Altman
remarks.role: AI is cool i guess
remarks.text:
we did not have our best last 12 months ever, which is mostly my fault, but we are about to have our best 12 months to date. the team is doing amazing work and i think you’ll be very happy with what they’ve got cooking for you.

i am happy about this for many reasons, but mostly because i care about our users winning. AI has to be about giving lots of people more freedom, agency, and wealth. we want to do the right thing, but we do not want to scare people into doing our thing.
remarks.translation:
中文释义：过去12个月并非我们表现最好的时期，这主要责任在我，但我们即将迎来迄今为止最出色的12个月。团队正在做出令人惊叹的工作，我想你会对他们为你准备的内容感到非常满意。我为此感到高兴，原因有很多，但最主要的是我在乎我们的用户取得成功。人工智能必须致力于赋予更多人自由、自主权和财富。我们想做正确的事，但我们不想通过恐吓让人们接受我们的方式。

保留信息：12m

remarks.name: Josh Woodward
remarks.role: VP, Google GoogleLabs GeminiApp GoogleAIStudio
remarks.text:
I still remember the exact room, watching @stevenbjohnson break down how he writes books. That was the spark for a little project we never imagined would grow this big.

Today, over 30 million people and 600,000 organizations use it, and it keeps growing. We all feel like it's just getting started.

For a while, we’ve just called it "Notebook" internally. Today, we're making it official externally. :)
remarks.translation:

remarks.name: Matt Turck
remarks.role: VC at FirstMarkCap.  Host: MAD Podcast; Organizer: Data Driven NYC, Author: MAD Landscape.
remarks.text:
"We can't build fast enough": my conversation with Sachin Katti (@sk7037), Head of Industrial Compute at @OpenAI about Stargate, Jalapeno, data center financing, and all things compute.

00:00 — Cold open: “One of the largest things humanity has ever built”

00:30 — Welcome: Sachin Katti, Head of Industrial Compute at OpenAI

01:44 — Is this the biggest infrastructure buildout in history? 03:41 — Why OpenAI is building a new industrial muscle

04:54 — What an AI data center actually is

05:27 — “Factories turning electrons into tokens”

06:35 — Why AI data centers need liquid cooling everywhere

08:10 — The power problem: grids, generation, transmission, substations

10:43 — Behind-the-meter power and gas turbines

11:02 — Why nuclear “can’t come soon enough”

11:49 — Jalapeño: why OpenAI is designing its own AI chips

13:19 — Tokens per watt: the new metric that matters

13:38 — Why inference may now dominate AI compute

14:58 — Is OpenAI overbuilding compute?

16:47 — Why OpenAI thinks the bigger risk is not building fast enough

17:55 — Communities, jobs, water, and the local data-center debate

21:16 — How OpenAI chooses data-center sites

22:25 — What “industrial compute” means inside OpenAI

25:59 — Sachin’s path: Stanford, startups, Intel, OpenAI

28:05 — OpenAI’s compute portfolio: Microsoft, hyperscalers, neoclouds

29:37 — Stargate explained

31:21 — Abilene, Oracle, and the next wave of AI data centers

32:48 — How massive AI compute gets financed

34:05 — How OpenAI designed Jalapeño so quickly

35:59 — AI is starting to help design AI chips

36:20 — MRC: the networking problem behind 100,000 GPUs

38:47 — Bottlenecks: transformers, turbines, electricians, supply chains

40:29 — Guaranteed capacity: intelligence as a supply unit

42:08 — Will AI data centers move to space?
remarks.translation:
中文释义：“我们建得还不够快”：我与OpenAI工业计算主管Sachin Katti（@sk7037）的对话，探讨Stargate、Jalapeno、数据中心融资以及所有与计算相关的话题。 00:00 — 开场白：“人类有史以来建造的最大事物之一” 00:30 — 欢迎：Sachin Katti，OpenAI工业计算主管 01:44 — 这是历史上规模最大的基础设施建设吗？ 03:41 — 为什么OpenAI正在打造新的工业实力 04:54 — AI数据中心究竟是什么 05:27 — “将电子转化为token的工厂” 06:35 — 为什么AI数据中心需要全面采用液冷 08:10 — 电力问题：电网、发电、输电、变电站 10:43 — 表后电源与燃气轮机 11:02 — 为什么核能“来得再快也不为过” 11:49 — Jalapeño：为什么OpenAI正在设计自己的AI芯片 13:19 — 每瓦token数：新的关键指标 13:38 — 为什么推理现在可能主导AI计算 14:58 — OpenAI是否在过度建设计算能力？ 16:47 — 为什么OpenAI认为更大的风险是建得不够快 17:55 — 社区、就业、水资源与本地数据中心争议 21:16 — OpenAI如何选择数据中心选址 22:25 — “工业计算”在OpenAI内部的含义 25:59 — Sachin的历程：斯坦福、创业、英特尔、OpenAI 28:05 — OpenAI的计算组合：微软、超大规模云商、新云商 29:37 — Stargate详解 31:21 — 阿比林、甲骨文与下一波AI数据中心 32:48 — 大规模AI计算如何融资 34:05 — OpenAI如何如此迅速地设计出Jalapeño 35:59 — AI开始辅助设计AI芯片 36:20 — MRC：10万块GPU背后的网络难题 38:47 — 瓶颈：变压器、涡轮机、电工、供应链 40:29 — 保障容量：智能作为供应单元 42:08 — AI数据中心会搬到太空吗？

保留信息：@OpenAI 100000
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Here’s another awesome use-case for what we can now do with our unstructured data because of AI agents. 

Box now works with Databricks so you can take structured data from enterprise content (like contracts, financial document, supply chain data) and connect that data into Databricks. 

This means that I can now query large document datasets without moving or reprocessing that content. And you can connect the data with any other system, like your ERP data, CRM, or product analytics. This opens up a ton of new use-cases for enterprise content. 

All possible because of headless software and agents.
remarks.translation: 这是AI代理让我们能够利用非结构化数据实现的另一个绝佳用例。Box现已与Databricks集成，您可以从企业内容（如合同、财务文件、供应链数据）中提取结构化数据，并将其接入Databricks。这意味着我现在无需移动或重新处理这些内容，就能查询海量文档数据集。您还可以将这些数据与任何其他系统连接，例如ERP数据、CRM或产品分析系统。这为企业内容开辟了大量全新用例。这一切都得益于无头软件和AI代理。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.text: Inkling: Our open-weights model
remarks.content: Inkling: Our open-weights model Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab just released their first open-weights model. Inkling is "a Mixture-of-Experts transformer with 975B total parameters, 41B active" - an Apac
remarks.contentTranslation: Inkling：我们的开放权重模型。Mira Murati的Thinking Machines Lab刚刚发布了他们的首个开放权重模型。Inkling是“一个总参数975B、活跃参数41B的混合专家（MoE）Transformer模型”——来自亚太地区。
remarks.translation: Inkling：我们的开放权重模型

podcasts.title: 为什么前沿生态系统必须开放——Matei Zaharia 与 Reynold Xin，Databricks

podcasts.excerpt: 我们非常高兴地欢迎Databricks加入AIEWF，与AI工程师生态系统中数百家顶尖公司齐聚一堂。LS订阅用户可使用专属折扣，在晚鸟价基础上再享优惠，并获得价值超过$50k的赞助商礼遇！大家仍在热议Satya的前沿生态系统文章，但真正构建出（<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…

LS subscribers can use their discount to get past the late bird pricing and access over $50k in sponsor offers! Everyone is still talking about Satya’s Frontier Ecosystems post, but few have actually built a (<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: 构建一所让AI模型学习人性的学校
podcasts.originalTitle: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
podcasts.excerpt: 如果规模定律持续成立——Surge AI 首席执行官埃德温·陈相信它们确实如此——我们正飞速冲向一个未来，在那里，人类能做的任何事，AI 都能做得更好。当 OpenAI 的模型运用新颖的代数几何技术，推翻了数学家保罗·埃尔德什提出的一个公开猜想时，菲尔兹奖得主蒂莫西·高尔斯敏锐地感受到了这种转变。他起初以为模型证明了一个上界，并做好了心理准备：那将意味着“数学家很快就要完蛋了”。当他意识到它只是找到了一个反例时，他松了一口气——这为他毕生投入的事业被 AI 超越又争取了一两年时间。作为为各大模型公司提供训练模型所用的数据环境和评估的公司创始人和首席执行官，陈对 AI 模型吸收那些我们曾视为人类独有任务的速度有着独特的视角……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: If scaling laws hold—and Surge AI CEO Edwin Chen believes they do—we’re hurtling toward a future where there’s nothing humans can do that AI can’t do better. When OpenAI’s models disproved an open conjecture posed by mathematician Paul Erdős using novel algebraic geometry techniques, Fields medalist Timothy Gowers felt the shift acutely. He initially thought the model had proved an upper bound, and braced himself: that would mean it was “all over for mathematicians very soon.” When he realized it had only found a counterexample, he was relieved—it bought him another year or two before the thing he’s devoted his life to becomes something AI does better.As founder and CEO of the company behind the data environments and evals the major model companies use to train their models, Chen has a unique perspective on how quickly AI models are absorbing tasks we used to think of as uniqu…

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: 🔬 自动驾驶实验室——约瑟夫·克劳斯，激进人工智能
podcasts.originalTitle: 🔬 The Self-Driving Lab — Joseph Krause, Radical AI
podcasts.excerpt: 在《科学播客》中，我们已广泛探讨了人工智能如何革新STEM领域，但自节目开播以来，我们私下最爱讨论的话题之一就是：数学、生物学和物理学中，哪个领域更难实现加速发展？今天，我们重返材料科学领域，与Radical一同探讨——与可通过标记字符串表示（甚至预测！）的生物分子不同，材料的成功涉及更多宏观复杂变量，如供应链、微观结构和制造工艺。还记得2023年的LK99风波吗……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: On the Science pod, we’ve been covering a lot of the ground on how AI is revolutionizing STEM, but one of our favorite off the record topics since our launch is which field is harder to accelerate: math, bio, or physics? Today we’re back in Materials Science land with Radical — Unlike biological molecules that can be represented (and predicted!) by token strings, the success of materials involve many more macro complex variables like supply chains, microstructures, and manufacturing processes. If you recall the LK99 drama of 2023…
